name: ddd
description: Domain-Driven Design: bounded contexts, aggregates, repositories as interfaces.

rules.guidance: Each bounded context has its own domain model and ubiquitous language. Do not share domain entities across contexts; translate at the anti-corruption layer.
Aggregates enforce consistency within their boundary. External code accesses aggregates only through their root. IDs are the only cross-aggregate references allowed.
Repositories are interfaces in the domain layer; infrastructure provides implementations. Repository methods work with aggregate roots, not raw query results.
Class, method, and variable names must reflect the domain's ubiquitous language. Rename when domain experts use different terms — code and language must match.
Significant state transitions emit domain events. Events are named in past tense (OrderPlaced, PaymentConfirmed). Subscribers handle events without knowing the publisher.
